tasks_completed.notes: Products, categories, product_images tables with UUIDs. Research applied: store_id for multi-tenant scaling, indexes on filtered columns (category_id, price_cents). Full-text search index prepared. Helper functions for category tree and product with images.
public_read_admin_write pattern applied to all tables. Active products/categories readable by everyone, modifications restricted to admins. Service role bypass for seed scripts.

Faker.js generates 10k products, 50 categories with hierarchical structure (electronics/phones/smartphones). Batch inserts for performance. Includes product images (1-5 per product from picsum). Idempotent - clears data before seeding.

All routes include pagination (24/page), Zod validation, error handling per _config/api-conventions.json. Products support filters: category, price range, search, sort. Category tree includes product counts.
Entry point for seeding. Runs synthetic-data.ts with progress logging and next-steps guide.

technical_decisions.decision: store_id column added to products for future multi-tenant scaling
technical_decisions.reason: Research finding - easier to add now than migrate later
technical_decisions.tradeoffs: Slight storage overhead, enables white-label SaaS growth
Write performance slightly slower, read performance significantly faster

technical_decisions.decision: Price stored as INTEGER (cents) not DECIMAL
technical_decisions.reason: Avoids floating point precision issues, faster comparisons

technical_decisions.decision: Faker.js instead of real product data
technical_decisions.reason: Legal/ethical concerns with scraping, faker provides realistic test data

technical_decisions.decision: Batch inserts (1000 per batch) for 10k products
technical_decisions.reason: Prevents memory issues and Supabase API limits
technical_decisions.tradeoffs: Slightly slower than single transaction, more reliable

notes_for_next_agent.gamma: Beta schema ready for CI/CD. Migrations located at supabase/migrations/beta/*.sql. Seed script needs SERVICE_ROLE_KEY in environment to run. After Alpha auth merge, products API will work with auth middleware.
notes_for_next_agent.sec: RLS policies use public_read_admin_write pattern consistent with Alpha. All admin mutations require admin role check. Service role used for seeding bypasses RLS as intended.
notes_for_next_agent.arch: Schema includes: 3 main tables (products, categories, product_images), 8 indexes for performance, 2 helper functions (get_category_tree, get_product_with_primary_image). Store_id column enables future multi-tenant architecture.

commit_message: Beta: Product catalog schema - 10k products, 50 categories, API routes, seed scripts
next_steps: Run seed script: npx ts-node scripts/seed/run.ts (requires SERVICE_ROLE_KEY)
Test API endpoints with seeded data
Merge to main after Alpha auth PR merged
